Intelligence isn’t about parameter count. It’s about time.
preview_articles.summary: As AI models grow larger, they become less insightful, not more. To ensure that they continue to learn, we need to reduce their inference time.
preview_articles.author: Stefano Soatto

preview_articles.title: Anthropic acquires Vercept to advance Claude's computer use capabilities
Anthropic’s Responsible Scaling Policy: Version 3.0
Detecting and preventing distillation attacks
Media Authenticity Methods in Practice: Capabilities, Limitations, and Directions
preview_articles.summary: As synthetic media grows, verifying what’s real, and the origin of content, matters more than ever. Our latest report explores media integrity and authentication methods, their limits, and practical paths toward trustworthy provenance across images, audio, and video.
preview_articles.author: Eric Horvitz, Andrew Jenks, Jessica Young

preview_articles.title: Project Silica’s advances in glass storage technology
preview_articles.summary: Project Silica introduces new techniques for encoding data in borosilicate glass, as described in the journal Nature. These advances lower media cost and simplify writing and reading systems while supporting 10,000-year data preservation.
preview_articles.author: Richard Black

preview_articles.title: Why a 12-year-old forecasting paper has stood the test of time
preview_articles.summary: Amazon Scholar Aravind Srinivasan coauthored a 2014 paper about forecasting civil unrest in Latin America, which won a test-of-time award at KDD 2025.
preview_articles.author: Larry Hardesty

preview_articles.title: Simplicity and Complexity in Combinatorial Optimization
Reducing Government Costs and Increasing Access to Greenspaces in the United Kingdom with DINO
Hybrid neural–cognitive models reveal how memory shapes human reward learning
Rethinking imitation learning with Predictive Inverse Dynamics Models
preview_articles.summary: This research looks at why Predictive Inverse Dynamics Models often outperform standard Behavior Cloning in imitation learning. By using simple predictions of what happens next, PIDMs reduce ambiguity and learn from far fewer demonstrations.
preview_articles.author: Pallavi Choudhury, Lukas Schäfer, Chris Lovett, Katja Hofmann, Sergio Valcarcel Macua

preview_articles.title: Paza: Introducing automatic speech recognition benchmarks and models for low resource languages
preview_articles.summary: Microsoft Research unveils Paza, a human-centered speech pipeline, and PazaBench, the first leaderboard for low-resource languages. It covers 39 African languages and 52 models and is tested with communities in real settings.
preview_articles.author: Mercy Muchai, Kevin  Chege, Nick  Mumero, Stephanie Nyairo

preview_articles.title: How academic collaboration delivers real-world security to Amazon customers
preview_articles.summary: An early meeting between Amazon scientists and Stanford researchers led to cvc5, an open-source tool now powering approximately one billion automated-reasoning checks across AWS every day.
preview_articles.author: Staff writer

preview_articles.title: Amazon Nova AI Challenge returns with Nova Forge access for competing teams
preview_articles.summary: For the first time in an academic competition, students can customize frontier AI models to build trusted software agents
preview_articles.author: Staff writer

preview_articles.title: Engaging the AI community through building, research, and shared learning
preview_articles.summary: Advancing AI requires more than breakthrough models. It depends on communities of builders and researchers who experiment, test assumptions, and share what they learn. That belief is guiding how Amazon engages developers and academics around Amazon Nova, Amazon’s portfolio of AI offerings including the Nova models, Nova Forge and Nova Act.
preview_articles.author: Staff writer

preview_articles.title: GSPO: Towards Scalable Reinforcement Learning for Language Models
preview_articles.summary: PAPER DISCORD Introduction Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a pivotal paradigm for scaling language models and enhancing their deep reasoning and problem-solving capabilities. To scale RL, the foremost prerequisite is maintaining stable and robust training dynamics. However, we observe that existing RL algorithms (such as GRPO) exhibit severe instability issues during long training and lead to irreversible model collapse, hindering further performance improvements with increased com...

preview_articles.title: Time to Speak Some Dialects, Qwen-TTS!

Trained on a large-scale dataset encompassing over millions of hours of speech, Qwen-TTS achieves human-level naturalness and expressiveness. Notably, Qwen-TTS automatically adjusts prosody, pacing, and emotional inflections in response to the input text.
